INQUIRER.net Apr 2, 03:06 PM

Indonesia issues new summons for Google, Meta over teen social media ban

JAKARTA, Indonesia — Indonesia issued a second round of summons letters on Thursday to Google and Meta for allegedly flouting a days-old ban on social media access for under-16s. The Communication and Digital Ministry said it had “issued a second summons letter to Google (YouTube) and Meta (Facebook, Instagram, Threads) after both parties failed to comply” and warned of “sanctions”. READ: Indonesia enforces social media ban for children under 16 App-addicted Indonesia began enforcing a social media ban for users under the age of 16 on Saturday, citing threats from online pornography, cyberbullying and internet addiction. The ministry said Thursday […]... Keep on reading: Indonesia issues new summons for Google, Meta over teen social media ban

INQUIRER.net Apr 2, 02:42 PM

April 2026 K-dramas: From ‘Perfect Crown’ to ‘Yumi’s Cells’ Season 3

The K-drama lineup for April 2026 is as intense as the heat in the Philippines, covering power struggles within a modern-day royal family, uncovering the mystery behind an illegal boxing syndicate, and navigating the toughest parts of office politics.  This time, it’s IU, Byeon Woo-seok, Kim Go-eun, and Kim Hyang-gi taking center stage. Many fans know what they’re capable of in their past work, and since their latest work shows them as different characters, it’s interesting to see how they’ll pull them off. From dealing with chaebol drama to coming to terms with one’s emotions, here’s a look at the […]... Keep on reading: April 2026 K-dramas: From ‘Perfect Crown’ to ‘Yumi’s Cells’ Season 3

INQUIRER.net Apr 2, 02:38 PM

US stocks join global selloff as oil prices jump again

NEW YORK — Wall Street stocks tumbled early Thursday on the latest jump in oil prices as tough rhetoric between the United States and Iran lessened hopes of a Middle East war resolution. About 30 minutes into trading, the Dow Jones Industrial Average was down 1.0 percent at 46,108.16. The broad-based S&P 500 fell 0.9 percent to 6,516.01, while the tech-rich Nasdaq Composite Index shed 1.3 percent to 21,556.30. US stocks had risen the last two days after Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian appeared to open the door to a ceasefire, while US President Donald Trump scheduled an address to the […]... Keep on reading: US stocks join global selloff as oil prices jump again
